name: ATM Gristedes 544
description_short: Coinstar kiosk located at Gristedes grocery store offering coin-to-cash conversion, cryptocurrency purchases, eGift card exchanges, and digital money transfer services.
description_long:
Coinstar operates a network of self-service kiosks designed to provide consumers with convenient cash access and financial services at retail locations. This specific kiosk is housed within a Gristedes supermarket at 748 2nd Ave. in New York, NY 10017, making it accessible during regular grocery shopping hours. The company was recently acquired by Arctic Slope Regional Corporation (ASRC) as of February 2026 and has expanded partnerships with major financial institutions including U.S. Bank. Coinstar positions itself as a modern alternative to traditional banking services by eliminating the need to visit physical bank branches for common financial transactions.

The kiosk offers a comprehensive suite of services beyond basic coin conversion. Primary offerings include Coins to Cash conversion (the company's flagship service), eGift card exchanges from major retailers like Amazon, Starbucks, Apple, and Home Depot, cryptocurrency purchases through partnerships with CINQ by Coinstar and Coinme, Amazon Cash loading, charity donations through the Coins that Count program, and account transfer capabilities via Push to Card. The service accepts coins and bills (though this specific location shows "Accepts Bills: N"), and users can exchange currency for digital wallet funding or traditional bank deposits. Fees vary by location and service type, with some eGift card options advertised as fee-free.

This kiosk distinguishes itself through its integration of emerging financial technologies alongside traditional coin conversion. Unlike standard ATMs that only dispense currency, this Coinstar location bridges traditional retail banking with cryptocurrency access, making crypto purchases possible at a grocery store location without requiring a bank account or traditional financial credentials. The breadth of eGift card partnerships and the charity donation option add utility beyond pure cash conversion. The availability of cryptocurrency services (25+ crypto forms available) represents a notable differentiator in the broader ATM and cash access category.

However, potential users should note several limitations. The specific location does not accept bills for conversion—only coins—which restricts its utility compared to some competing Coinstar locations. All services carry variable fees that are not transparently displayed on the website, making cost comparison difficult. The kiosk's hours are limited to Gristedes operating hours, and reliance on cryptocurrency services requires smartphone apps and account setup in advance. While positioned as convenient, this remains a specialty kiosk better suited for specific transaction types rather than general-purpose cash access.
pros: Coin-to-cash conversion without manual counting, sorting, or rolling required
No-fee eGift card exchange options available at this location
Access to cryptocurrency purchases (25+ crypto options) at a grocery store without bank account requirement
Amazon Cash loading service allows shopping on Amazon.com without credit/debit cards
Direct charity donation capability through Coins that Count program supporting UNICEF
Account transfer via Push to Card enables direct deposits to checking accounts, bypassing bank visits
Convenient grocery store location at Gristedes integrated with shopping trips
cons: This specific location does NOT accept bills—only coins—severely limiting transaction utility compared to other Coinstar locations
All services subject to variable location-based fees that are not transparently listed, making cost assessment impossible before using the kiosk
Limited hours restricted to Gristedes supermarket operating schedule, not 24/7 access like traditional ATMs
Cryptocurrency and digital wallet services require advance smartphone app downloads and account setup before visiting the kiosk
Does not offer foreign currency exchange, limiting utility for travelers
best_for: Consumers with accumulated coins seeking quick cash conversion during grocery shopping trips
Early cryptocurrency adopters wanting accessible on-ramp to digital assets without traditional banking infrastructure
Amazon.com shoppers preferring cash-based payments without credit/debit card exposure
Individuals seeking to donate loose change to charitable causes while converting currency
diagnosis: This Coinstar kiosk is best for coin holders seeking grocery store-integrated cash conversion and individuals interested in cryptocurrency access without traditional banking, but is limited by its coins-only acceptance policy at this specific location. The variable, non-transparent fee structure and requirement for app-based signup for digital services reduce its appeal as a general-purpose cash access solution compared to standard ATMs.
services: eGift card exchanges (AMC, Apple, Starbucks, Home Depot, Domino's, DoorDash, Nike, and 10+ others)
Cryptocurrency purchase via CINQ by Coinstar
CINQ by Coinstar debit card services
Account transfer/Push to Card deposits to checking accounts
Charity donations through Coins that Count program
Digital wallet services for cryptocurrency storage and management
meta_description: Coinstar kiosk at Gristedes grocery store offering coin-to-cash conversion, cryptocurrency purchases, eGift cards, and digital money transfer.
